title: Within-lap ranking auxiliary model grouped by race-lap
summary: Pit calls are relative decisions among cars exposed to the same lap and race state.
rationale: Pit calls are relative decisions among cars exposed to the same lap and race state. Learning-to-rank objectives explicitly optimize ordering inside a group, which matches the competition’s same-lap candidate set better than pure independent binary loss. This develops the earlier ranking-head idea into a concrete test: rank cars within each `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` query, then blend with the binary model.
implementation_hint: Train a LightGBM `lambdarank` or CatBoost ranking model with `GroupId=(Year,Race,LapNumber)`. Use target `PitNextLap` as relevance, optionally upweight top positions in each group, generate OOF rank scores, and blend them with the binary classifier logits or probabilities.
expected_effect: Sharper separation among simultaneously eligible cars on the same lap, improving AUC on clustered pit-wave decisions.
risk: Some race-lap groups may have zero or many positives, so ranking alone can be unstable; treat it as an auxiliary expert, not a standalone replacement.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
